title: Besseya wyomingensis
taxonomy: Besseya wyomingensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is a member of the genus Besseya, which is a group of plants commonly known as Wyoming besseya or mountain boggs. The plant is native to the western United States and is often found growing in moist mountain meadows and rocky outcroppings. Besseya wyomingensis is a perennial herb that produces small, white or purple flowers in late spring and early summer. The plant's classification is further supported by its close relationships with other species within the Plantaginaceae family.
